name: Roasted Garlic-Stuffed Chicken Breasts
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Place garlic cloves in a small oven-proof skillet; cover with olive oil (add more oil if necessary to cover garlic). Heat until a simmer over medium-high heat.
3. Bake, covered, until garlic is lightly browned, about 20 minutes. Remove from oven and let cool. Reserve the oil.
4. Mash cooked garlic in a bowl with 1 teaspoon of the reserved oil until smooth.
5. Cut a pocket into each chicken breast; stuff with mashed garlic. Refrigerate up to 1 day if desired.
6. Preheat oven to 400°F.
7. Heat 2 tablespoons of the reserved oil in a medium skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
8. Stir in tomatoes, herbs de provence, salt, and pepper. Cook for 5 minutes.
9. Add broth; simmer over low heat for 15 minutes.
10. Heat 2 tablespoons of the reserved oil in an oven-proof skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken breasts, skin side up, and cook until browned on both sides.
11. Place skillet with chicken breasts in the oven. Bake until chicken is no longer pink in the center, about 8 to 10 minutes.
12. Remove chicken from pan and keep warm. Add wine to pan and heat to a boil over medium-high heat, scraping bottom of pan to remove any browned pieces.
13. Add wine and capers to tomato sauce; cook for 15 minutes to reduce and thicken the sauce. Pour sauce over chicken breasts and serve.
Test kitchen note: Skinless chicken breasts may be substituted. Cover breasts when baking to avoid dryness (using skin-on breasts: Cal 340, Fat 20 gr fat, 53% fat). Source: Christopher Koetke, chef at Les Nomades. The Chicago Tribune. MM Waldine Van Geffen vghc42a@prodigy.com. Lynn Thomas' notes: Made this in 10-13-97; used about 50 cloves of garlic and boneless, skinless chicken. Very delicious dish and very garlicky!
